pattern: Whether every asteroid can be destroyed depends only on the multiset of asteroid masses, not the order chosen — colliding with the smallest surviving asteroid first can never hurt and only grows the planet's mass sooner.
hints: The order in which the planet collides with asteroids is entirely up to you — think about which order can never make things worse.
Destroying the smallest available asteroid first only ever grows the planet's mass as early as possible, so try sorting the asteroids ascending and processing them in that order.

Sort the asteroids ascending and walk through them, accumulating mass as each is destroyed; the moment the planet's current mass is smaller than the next asteroid, destruction is impossible from that point on — return false immediately, otherwise return true once every asteroid has been processed.

edge_inputs.description: small ascending run, planet grows comfortably
single asteroid larger than the planet
single asteroid exactly equal to the planet's mass
many asteroids exactly equal to the starting mass — tests the >= boundary repeatedly
input order places a large asteroid before smaller ones that would unlock it if processed first
several equal mid-size asteroids that only become destructible after accumulating mass
maximum mass and asteroid value, exactly equal
minimum mass against the maximum possible asteroid value

stress.description: large random asteroid field at the max constraint size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    asteroids = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    mass = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    return [mass, asteroids]
